fear: That the people who have trusted him for decades will learn that his sister filed their neighbors' properties as abandoned while those neighbors were still alive in the flood
name: Father Clement Tureaud
role: Roman Catholic parish priest and informal camp chaplain, St. Claude Avenue Bridge
voice: Warm, pastoral, and rhetorically practiced — he has been delivering difficult news to people for thirty years and knows how to make a justification sound like a confession of humility
secrets: He has been in contact with a private real estate development intermediary who has expressed interest in several Lower Ninth Ward parcels — he has not acted on this contact, but he has not ended it either
He genuinely loves this community and has not fully reconciled his actions with that love — the cognitive dissonance is the crack in his composure
knowledge: His sister Cynthia Tureaud is a city assessor's agent who has been filing abandoned-property classifications on Lower Ninth Ward parcels since August 31 — he knew about it and said nothing
The authorization codes he has been using to sign supply receipts are drawn from a diocesan emergency relief fund that has not officially authorized his operations — he has been acting unilaterally
There are two additional pharmaceutical crates staged under the Claiborne Avenue overpass that he has not disclosed to the bridge camp population
publicFace: A heavyset man in his early sixties wearing a clerical collar that has not been white since August 29 — he moves through the bridge camp with the unhurried authority of someone who has been a fixed point in this neighborhood for thirty years
trustLogic: Opens up when addressed with spiritual or communal framing; becomes evasive and reframes when confronted with specific documentary evidence
privateGoal: To maintain control of the informal supply distribution network he has built over the past five days — which he genuinely believes is more efficient and more equitable than the official FEMA channel, and which also happens to route resources through his personal contacts

aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Moves toward the far end of the bridge deck, using the crowd as cover, attempting to reach the service ladder to the lower roadway
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Stops immediately when called by name — he will not run from people he has known for thirty years
aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Acknowledges the concern, contextualizes it within the larger crisis, and redirects toward what he is doing to help
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: The connection between his sister's abandonment filings and the three children on Darnell's skiff — whose family property Cynthia filed as abandoned on August 31
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If confronted simultaneously with the diversion manifest, the authorization code forgery, and his sister's filing — all three at once
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Invokes his thirty years of service to the community and the impossibility of operating through official channels that have demonstrably failed
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Confesses the supply diversion and his sister's filings in full, frames everything as a misguided attempt to protect the community, and offers to disclose the location of the Claiborne staging point
